Empires & Puzzles Heifeng Guai Review: The Black Wind Sniper

Heifeng Guai brings a unique trick to the 4-star arena: resetting the duration of status ailments. Combined with Fast speed and Defense Down, is he a sleeper hit or just a weaker version of other ailment refreshers?
Heifeng Guai: Fast Defense Down and Infinite Ailments
Heifeng Guai, The Black Wind Demon: Sniper with a Twist

Heifeng Guai is a 4-star Epic Dark hero from the Journey family, released in early 2023. He is a Rogue class sniper who brings a mix of direct damage, defense reduction, and the niche utility of resetting status ailments. While he lives in the shadow of his 5-star family members, he offers a unique tactical option for 4-star tournaments and events.
Heifeng Guai is depicted as a bear-like demon (based on the Black Bear Demon from

), wielding a black-tasseled spear. The art is solid, capturing his menacing yet somewhat disciplined nature. He looks like a warrior who means business, fitting well with the other "animal spirit" designs in the Journey family.
In late 2025, his stats are serviceable but not exciting.
A bit low for a sniper. He relies on his skill multiplier and debuff to deal damage.
These are decent survivability stats for a 4-star, especially with the Rogue dodge talent.
He falls into the mid-tier of 4-star power.
Special Skill: Black Tassel Spear Sting
"Black Tassel Spear Sting" is a combo platter.
285% damage to target. This is underwhelming. Most 4-star snipers hit for 300-400%+. He trades raw damage for utility.
"The duration of dispellable status ailments is reset for the target." This is his signature move. If the enemy is poisoned, burning, or mana-blocked, Heifeng Guai resets the timer to full.
-35% defense for 3 turns. This is a standard, solid debuff. It helps your team deal more damage, somewhat compensating for his weak initial hit.
. 8 tiles. This is critical. He needs to fire frequently to keep resetting ailments and applying defense down.
Compared to a classic sniper like Tiburtus (Costumed) or newer hard hitters, this is soft. You bring him for the effects, not the kill shot.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The chance to extend status effect duration works well with his kit. If he extends his own Defense Down to 4 or 5 turns, he becomes much more valuable.
. Essential for helping his weak snipe deal at least some noticeable damage.
Upon release, he was compared unfavorably to Zhabog (S5 4-star), who resets ailments on
As a single-target ailment resetter with low damage, he has struggled to find a permanent home. The game has moved towards AoE effects.

. He is useful in specific 4-star challenges where you need to keep a boss permanently locked down with Proteus or Merlin, using Heifeng Guai to refresh the duration. Outside of that, there are usually better Dark 4-stars (C.Rigard, C.Tiburtus, Sergei, etc.).
As of this review, Heifeng Guai does not have a costume.
Keeps him alive to maintain the debuff loop.
Tries to make him a threat.
Still won't hit very hard compared to true snipers.
. His primary role is utility (Defense Down + Reset). If he dies, the combo breaks.
Always useful in titan teams or raid stacks.
Can extend powerful effects like Mindless Attack or Mana Stop indefinitely on a single target.
285% is very low for a sniper.
Dark has incredible 4-stars (Sergei, Tiburtus, Rigard, Sabina, Proteus). He struggles to compete for a roster spot.
Resetting DoT or Mana Control on a boss can be a winning strategy.
Fast defense down is good if you don't have C.Tiburtus or Cheshire Cat.
Usable in fast/buff booster settings as a debuffer.
Proteus stops mana. Before it expires, Heifeng Guai hits the target to reset the duration to full (3 turns). Rinse and repeat.
, but he needs someone else to apply the ailments he resets. He is a support hero, not a carry.
If he is your first Dark 4-star with Defense Down, level him immediately. Defense Down is the most important debuff in the game. He will serve you well until you get Tiburtus or Sergei.
He is likely food or a deep bench hero. Unless you lack Zhabog and really want to play with ailment resetting mechanics, he doesn't offer much to a developed roster.
The most common community sentiment. Why reset one when you can reset all?
Players were disappointed by the 285% damage. "Is he tickling them?"

Heifeng Guai is a hero with a cool mechanic trapped in a mediocre stat block and scope. Being single-target limits his "ailment refresh" potential compared to his peers. However, having a Fast Defense Down hero is never a bad thing for a developing roster.
A serviceable 4-star who fills gaps but rarely shines as the star of the show.
The Black Wind blows... and your poison lasts forever.
